overview.short: Shivoham Shiva Temple is a Hindu temple, situated on Old Airport Road, Bangalore, India, dedicated to the Hindu deity Shiva.
overview.description: Shivoham Shiva Temple is a Hindu temple, situated on Old Airport Road, Bangalore, India, dedicated to the Hindu deity Shiva. Built in 1995, it features a 65 feet (20 m) statue of Shiva. The temple attracts an estimated 500,000 worshippers and visitors each year, including between 100,000 and 150,000 on the occasion of Maha Shivaratri.

topPlaces.name: U. R. Rao Satellite Centre
topPlaces.description: The U. R. Rao Satellite Centre (URSC), formerly ISRO Satellite Centre (ISAC) is an ISRO centre for the design, development, and construction of Indian satellites. It was established in 1972 as Indian Scientific Satellite Project (ISSP) in Peenya Industrial Estates of Bengaluru.

topPlaces.name: Jeevanbimanagar
topPlaces.description: Jeevanabimanagara, also known incorrectly as Jeevan Bhima Nagar is a residential area of east Bangalore. The area extends from the Indiranagara 80 Feet Road in the west to Suranjandas Road in the east.

topPlaces.name: Murugeshpalya
topPlaces.description: Murugeshpalya is a neighbourhood in Bengaluru, India.

topPlaces.name: NAL Wind Tunnel Road
topPlaces.description: NAL Wind Tunnel Road, expanded as National Aerospace Laboratories Wind Tunnel Road, is a road in the Murugeshpalya locality of Bengaluru. It was called Challaghatta Road, a name that still persists in local use, before it was renamed.

topPlaces.name: Vimanapura
topPlaces.description: Vimanapura or Baiyyappanahalli Vimanapura is an eastern suburb of Bangalore in Karnataka in India. It is so called because it is near Bangalore HAL airport.

topPlaces.name: Indiranagar, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Indiranagar is an affluent and luxury neighbourhood in east Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. Indiranagar is one of the most expensive cosmopolitan neighbourhoods in Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Ashraya
topPlaces.description: Ashraya is an Indian non governmental organisation headquartered at Indiranagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India, working for the welfare of destitute children and empowerment of women.

topPlaces.name: Indian Airlines Flight 605
topPlaces.description: Indian Airlines Flight 605 was a scheduled domestic passenger flight from Bombay to Bangalore. On 14 February 1990, an Airbus A320-231 registered as VT-EPN, crashed onto a golf course while attempting to land at Bangalore, killing 92 of 146 people on board.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — U. R. Rao Satellite Centre & around

items.note: The U. R. Rao Satellite Centre (URSC), formerly ISRO Satellite Centre (ISAC) is an ISRO centre for the design, development, and construction of Indian satellites. It was established in 1972 as Indian Scientific Satellite Project (ISSP) in Peenya Industrial Estates of Bengaluru.

Jeevanabimanagara, also known incorrectly as Jeevan Bhima Nagar is a residential area of east Bangalore. The area extends from the Indiranagara 80 Feet Road in the west to Suranjandas Road in the east.
Murugeshpalya is a neighbourhood in Bengaluru, India.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — NAL Wind Tunnel Road & around

items.note: NAL Wind Tunnel Road, expanded as National Aerospace Laboratories Wind Tunnel Road, is a road in the Murugeshpalya locality of Bengaluru. It was called Challaghatta Road, a name that still persists in local use, before it was renamed.
Vimanapura or Baiyyappanahalli Vimanapura is an eastern suburb of Bangalore in Karnataka in India. It is so called because it is near Bangalore HAL airport.
Indiranagar is an affluent and luxury neighbourhood in east Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. Indiranagar is one of the most expensive cosmopolitan neighbourhoods in Bangalore.
Ashraya is an Indian non governmental organisation headquartered at Indiranagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India, working for the welfare of destitute children and empowerment of women.

Indian Airlines Flight 605 was a scheduled domestic passenger flight from Bombay to Bangalore. On 14 February 1990, an Airbus A320-231 registered as VT-EPN, crashed onto a golf course while attempting to land at Bangalore, killing 92 of 146 people on board.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (27 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple?
faq.a: Stays in The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in The RVM Foundation Shiv Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are U. R. Rao Satellite Centre, Jeevanbimanagar, Murugeshpalya, NAL Wind Tunnel Road, Vimanapura.
